food_name: Beef Topside, Raw, Lean, Trimmed of Visible Fat
food_subgroup: Beef topside (inner thigh), raw, lean, trimmed of visible fat

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical raw serving of beef topside is approximately 100–150g, equivalent to a palm-sized piece of lean beef before cooking.

nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore; USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values from HPB Singapore Foodo Database Food Code F06030020 (exact match). Cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy entry for beef round, top round, separable lean only, raw, for secondary micronutrient validation. All values consistent between sources.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — Beef Topside, Raw, Lean contains 0.0g carbohydrate per 100g; no meaningful glycemic response; GL per serving is 0.0 by definition
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is not applicable for this food. Beef topside raw lean contains 0.0g carbohydrate per 100g. Pure protein and fat foods do not elicit a meaningful glycemic response and are not assigned a GI value by the University of Sydney GI Database. GI is set to 0 as a numeric sentinel indicating no glycemic response; gi_category is 'not_applicable'. GL per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (56mg/100g) and low saturated fat (1.6g/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Beef topside raw lean is suitable for hypertensive patients in moderate portions (100–150g per serving, 3–4 times per week) given its low sodium content of 56mg/100g and relatively low saturated fat of 1.6g/100g; preparation method should avoid added salt or high-sodium marinades to maintain cardiovascular benefit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate content; high-quality protein with haem iron, zinc, and vitamin B12
type2_diabetes.guidance: Beef topside raw lean contains 0.0g carbohydrate per 100g and will not raise blood glucose; it provides 20.7g high-quality protein, 4.5mg zinc, and 2.4mcg vitamin B12 per 100g, addressing common micronutrient deficiencies in type 2 diabetes, though consumption should be limited to no more than 3–4 servings per week given epidemiological associations between regular red meat intake and increased type 2 diabetes risk.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (1.6g/100g) and cholesterol (62mg/100g); lean cut with favourable protein-to-fat ratio
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Beef topside raw lean has a moderate cardiovascular concern level due to its saturated fat content of 1.6g/100g and cholesterol of 62mg/100g; however, as one of the leaner beef cuts available, it is preferable to higher-fat cuts such as ribeye or brisket, and cardiovascular patients may include it in a heart-healthy diet at 1–2 servings per week when prepared without added saturated fats.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High zinc (4.5mg/100g) supports immune function; modest pro-inflammatory potential of red meat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Beef topside raw lean provides 4.5mg zinc and 2.4mcg vitamin B12 per 100g, both important for immune function and neurological health in rheumatoid arthritis patients; however, lean red meat consumption is associated with modest increases in inflammatory markers and should be limited to 2–3 servings per week in RA management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef topside (also known as top round or inner round) is a lean cut of beef taken from the inner thigh (round) of the hindquarter of cattle. In its raw, trimmed state, it displays a deep red colour with minimal visible marbling, as all separable external and internal fat has been removed. Per 100g raw, it provides 127 kcal, 20.7g high-quality complete protein, 4.0g total fat (of which only 1.6g is saturated fat), 0.1g trans fat, and 0.0g carbohydrate, making it one of the leanest beef cuts available. It is an exceptional source of zinc (4.5mg/100g — among the best non-shellfish zinc sources), haem iron (2.1mg/100g, with 15–35% bioavailability compared to 2–5% for plant-based non-haem iron), and vitamin B12 (2.4mcg/100g, critical for neurological function and red blood cell formation). Selenium content is notable at 18.6mcg/100g. Sodium is low at 56mg/100g in the raw state. In Singapore, beef topside is used in beef pho (Vietnamese-style noodle soup), stir-fried beef with vegetables, beef steak, and occasionally beef curry; beef rendang traditionally uses chuck or brisket rather than topside. Nutritionally, it is a preferred lean red meat choice for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease when consumed in moderate portions (100–150g, 2–4 times per week). The HPB Singapore Foodo Database lists this food under code F06030020 as a canonical reference lean beef cut.
common_user_queries: how much beef is healthy per week

beef rendang: traditionally uses chuck or brisket, not topside

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo Database Food Code F06030020 (exact match, verified provenance, confidence 1.0), cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy for beef round, top round, separable lean only, raw. All core macros and micronutrients are consistent between both approved primary sources. Ontology codes (FoodOn, FoodEx2) are exact matches. No estimation or analogy required for nutrient values.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as this is an animal muscle food. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is an animal muscle food containing no plant fibre. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae and vitamin_c_mg are confirmed zero per HPB F06030020 source data. Monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data not separately reported in HPB Foodo Database F06030020; USDA SR Legacy reports approximately 1.5g MUFA and 0.15g PUFA for comparable top round lean raw, but these values were not adopted as the primary HPB source does not report them and cross-source imputation is not applied under Veritas source policy. Water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo Database F06030020 primary record. Glycemic index is set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value indicating no glycemic response; gi_category is 'not_applicable' as this food contains 0.0g carbohydrate per 100g; GL per serving is 0.0 by definition. Halal status reflects that beef topside is inherently halal when sourced from halal-certified abattoirs; kosher status not confirmed. Hindi local name not populated as no standard Hindi culinary term for beef topside is in common use in the Singapore context.
